channel.title: Comments for page "Ad Astra Per Aspera Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Ad Astra Per Aspera Hub"

item.title: Re: Music?
item.content:encoded: Sevish's music isn't under creative commons and, as the canon organizer, I don't see any point in having music file. Sure, it could add to the mood, but it isn't something I'd consider a necessity.

item.title: Music?
item.content:encoded: I was thinking of having a music file on the page to set the mood, similar to the AIAD canon. I want to use the song "Ganymede" by Sevish, but I'm not sure if that's allowed. Thoughts?

item.title: UPDATE
item.content:encoded:
The hub has been revised to include links to all International Branch AAPA content! Any writers from Int branches are free to include links to their own works and any translations, if they exist. If there's any overall edits for the tab you think could be good just let me know.

UPD8: I have also increased the timeline cap from 2140 to 2170 (inclusive) because I need an excuse to include more batshit worldbuilding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What stormbreath said; that and this ordering places the primary importance on "to the stars" as the canon's stylistic focus, followed by "through hardship" as the underlying narrative and thematic focus. Connotations of excitement and discovery resting above the connotations of pain and struggle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Word order in Latin is a lot looser than English, so either arrangement of the saying is equally valid. Per Aspera Ad Astra is more common, but they mean the same thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "To the stars through hardship” vs. "Through hardship to the stars".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really love this canon. I know how I'll be spending my day.
Also, I always thought it was Per Aspera Ad Astra, not the other way around.
I'm never changing that reserved post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*opens up the top of my skull like a box lid and crams all this excellent space fiction inside*

IMO, this canon also has the most lovely and pleasant-to-read color scheme I've seen on the site, so kudos to the designer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the Eclipse Phase vibes from this canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus that's a lot of articles, where did all this come from? I'm gonna have to take a day or two sometime soon and binge this, since the concept and authors seem right up my alley.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohhhh I've never actually heard of that before. I'll check it out when I get the chance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Space Fantasia 2001 is a Japanese SF manga by Hoshino Yukinobu. It was seriously affected by 2001 Space Odyssey by Clarke, though has quite different imagery. I'm certain that you must be like it if you love Odyssey.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, nice to see that style shows through. I'd be lying if I said 2001 and Arthur C. Clarke's style of sci-fi as a whole hasn't influenced my writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have not read all of articles yet, but this hub itself deserve +1.
This reminds me nice and old hard SF thingys including Space Fantasia 2001. Space is always good and legitimate.

item.title: Re: Canon connections
item.content:encoded: That's the case. Smaller scale events from individual articles could be canon to AAPA, but that's dependent on a lot of other factors with AAPA's content so it isn't set in stone.

item.title: Canon connections
item.content:encoded:
Happy to see this hub up finally.

I do have one question related to the “related continuities” section. With respect to Third Law, the only real connections to it, besides the tech, are the general events correct? Things like the AOI-Obskura conflict and the rise and fall of Prometheus Labs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent to finally see this up! It was very clearly a labour of love on your part, and it definitely shows; you done did good with this one, Nat.

item.title: Omedetou! ¡Felicidades!
item.content:encoded:
Like Simon, I'm a loyal fan of Ad Astra from Spanish branch. I waited patiently for seeing this live. The whole world needs to see this!

Congratulations!

A (non) fitting song for this posting

item.title: Great pleasure and Congratulations ~\(^o^)/~
item.content:encoded:
As a translator and reader from Chinese branch and a loyal fan of Broken Masquerade Canon and Ad Astra Per Aspera Canon. I'd Focus on the sandbox page of AAPA since Nov 2018. It's my great pleasure and honor to be able to witness the releasing of AAPA.

I would like to offer my sincere congratulations to you!

It's my honor to have chance to translate the AAPA HUB into Chinese, and now this work was done, I will updating the Chinese AAPA HUB whenever you updated the original page.

Thanks for all of you! Thanks for all you’ve done! ~\(^o^)/~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is a big brain canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We've certainly come a long way from Peanut.

Personally, I like this canon. It seems less existentially devastating than End of Death.

item.title: +1 to infinity and beyond
item.content:encoded:
IT'S UP! \o/

I have to say, Ad Astra is an absolute BLAST to read and contribute to! This is, of course, due to the solid writing and diligence on the part of its many contributors; the wealth of science fiction/space opera concepts and narratives to choose from; and the hard work of Nat for arranging everything and tying it all together, and acting as an ever-present source of ideas and feedback along the way. It's really thrilling to see this up and I can't wait to see what people contribute in the future.

+1 to infinity and beyond to the stars through hardship!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
IT'S FINALLY HERE!

Seeing this slowly coming together for the last many months has been great, all the countless hours of many authors for the past who knows how long is now in one place for all to enjoy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reserved.

Image credit:

  • Spacewalk
  • Habitable Mars
  • O'Neill Cylinders
  • Carina Nebula
